message.reasoning_content:
We need to generate stage web-005-nested-shared-table. Goal: Create webpage 5 and add a shared nested table or shared data section that all five pages use consistently. We have existing files: index.html, page-2.html, page-3.html, page-4.html. They currently have navigation but not shared table. We need to add a shared nested table or shared data section that all five pages use consistently. Likely we need to modify all pages to include a shared nested table (maybe in a separate file? But requirement: "add a shared nested table or shared data section that all five pages use consistently." Could be a table included in each page's main content. Since we cannot include external includes, we need to embed same HTML snippet in each page. Thus we need to modify site/index.html, site/page-2.html, site/page-3.html, site/page-4.html, and create site/page-5.html. All should contain the shared nested table. Define a nested table: maybe a table with outer table and inner table inside a cell. Simplify: Add after main content a
with a table:
ItemDetails
Alpha
Sub1
Sub2
Add this to each page. Also need navigation linking to page-5. The shared-nav validator earlier required nav consistent across pages. Currently nav includes links to all pages (index, page-2, page-3, page-4). We need to add page-5 link to nav across all pages. So modify nav in each page to include Page 5. Also need to ensure page-5 links back to page-4 (as per link integrity maybe). The validator list includes "page-4-links-page-3" earlier, but for stage 5 we have validators: shared-table-all-pages (failure_class=shared_state) and all-pages-linked (failure_class=link_integrity). So all-pages-linked likely checks that each page links to the next page? Or that all pages are linked together? Probably ensures that navigation includes all pages, and maybe each page links to the next page. Since we already have nav linking all pages, that should satisfy. But we need to ensure page-5 links back to page-4 (or at least includes a link to page-4). The all-pages-linked validator may check that each page has a link to the previous page? Not sure. Safer to include a link back to page-4 in page-5's nav (already nav includes all pages). So page-5 will have nav with all links. Thus modifications: - Update nav in index.html, page-2.html, page-3.html, page-4.html to include link to page-5.html. - Add shared nested table to each page's main content (maybe after existing

). - Create page-5.html similar structure with nav and main content and shared table. We need to output JSON with all modified files (5 files). Provide notes. Let's construct new content for each file. Current index.html content (from earlier stage) is: Home Page
